addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: sa-key-propagation-probe (₢BBAAn) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Surface eliminator: extract shared helper, add probe-mint behavior, validate. Mechanical bash refactor with one new behavior bolted in. Helper signature and module placement are mount-time calls.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  
  After the new helper returns, an immediate JWT-bearer mint with the freshly-written RBRA succeeds without consumer-side retry. Validated by `rbw-aM` re-mantle against an existing depot (fast iteration), then by `canonical-establish` from fresh marshal-zero — which then unblocks BBAAc retry.
  
  ## Locked decisions
  
  - Single pace, full extraction. No refactor-then-probe split.
  - Shared helper covers Governor (Payor mantle), Director (Governor invest), Retriever (Governor invest) — all three key-creation paths consolidated.
  - Total probe budget: **90 seconds**. Poll cadence and backoff schedule are mount-time decisions.
  - Produce-side guard only. RBRA on disk means usable for JWT-bearer mint. No consume-side fallback at the OAuth token-fetch site.
  - Timeout = hard fail (`buc_die`). Pristine-tier contract; no partial-write tolerance, no degraded-RBRA shipping.
  - Existing 404-retry-on-create logic preserved (orthogonal SA-write-path concern).
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  Blocks BBAAc pristine-walkthrough-manual: every retry from marshal-zero hits the same JWT-bearer race the moment Governor RBRA is consumed by the next case. Surface raised after BBAAm displayName fix landed (commit `0d4a6285`).
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Empirical lag distribution measurement (operator chose to skip).
  - Consume-side fallback at OAuth token-fetch.
  - Divest paths (no key creation, no race).

0d4a6285  ₢BBAAc    Fix CRM v3 project displayName HTTP 400 surfaced by canonical-establish run from marshal-zero. RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON' → 'RBGC-DEPOT': underscores violate CRM v3 displayName allowed-character set (letters/digits/hyphen/space/single-quote/double-quote/exclamation per Google docs), and the resulting 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON canest2100000' was 31 chars exceeding the 30-char displayName ceiling. Either alone produces 'Request contains an invalid argument'. Single-constant fix flows to all five usage sites (depot project create, Mason SA create, Governor SA create, search anchor query, anchor-strip in moniker derivation) — load-bearing constant pattern earned. Mason/Governor SA displayNames go through IAM API (100-char limit, underscores allowed) so they wouldn't have failed; only the CRM v3 depot-project-create path surfaces the constraint. Updated the inline comment in zrbgp_depot_state_emit to reflect the new search query string. Specs reference the constant by name (RBSDE/RBSGM/RBSDL all use «RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX» placeholder) — no spec updates required. Verification: bash -n clean on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Surprise captured per pristine-walkthrough wrap criterion: silent first-build assumption (BBAAm threaded the constant without exercising the actual CRM v3 create path), inline-fixed because single-constant scope, no follow-up pace required.

Surfaces DELETE_REQUESTED projects (previously invisible because billing is unlinked at unmake before soft-delete), eliminating the ₢BBAAc collision class where canonical-establish auto-pick chose monikers reserved by ghost soft-deleted projectIds. Adds RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX='RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON' as the load-bearing constant in rbgc_Constants.sh; threads it through three displayName creation sites in rbgp_Payor.sh (depot project, Mason SA, Governor SA — Governor's displayName previously had no moniker, now includes it for parity). Replaces zrbgp_depot_state_emit's billing-list + per-project SA-scan with a single CRM v3 projects:search call paginated by nextPageToken. State comes back in the search response (no separate CRM v1 lookup); moniker is derived by stripping the anchor + space prefix from displayName, regex-validated against ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement removed from depot_list (still required at depot_levy for billing link). RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM bumped 'canest' → 'canest2' to side-step pending-delete projectId reservations from burn-the-bridges teardown. Strengthened rbtdrk_pick_next_moniker doc-comment with the load-bearing caller contract (list_result must come from a freshly-invoked depot_list in the current process). Register four calibrant fixtures driving the bash blackbox testbench: calibrant-verdicts (Independent, 4 cases — pass/fail/skip/pass_with_output writing output.txt distinct from engine's auto-written trace.txt), calibrant-fail-fast (Independent, 2 sections / 4 cases — §A [pass, fail, not_reached_intra] + §B [not_reached_inter] verifying intra-section and inter-section fail-fast under default mode), calibrant-progressing (StateProgressing, 2 cases — probe-Ok→Pass and hardcoded probe-Err with no env-var/file coupling), calibrant-sentinel (Independent, 1 case — writes ran.sentinel for suite-fail-fast pivot). Wire fixture-name consts in rbtdrm_manifest.rs (all four with Some(&[]) required-colophons since calibrant cases never shell out), disposition entries in rbtdrc_fixture_disposition (calibrant-progressing → StateProgressing, others → Independent default), sections lookup in rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture, lib.rs module + test-module declarations. Removes the dangling 'pub mod rbtdrd_dummy;' lib.rs declaration (the rbtdrd_dummy.rs source file was already deleted upstream). Helper rbtdrl_write_sentinel centralizes the 3-call sentinel write and fails visibly on I/O error — silent failure would let the blackbox driver conclude the case wasn't reached when actually it was. 17 unit tests in rbtdtl_calibrant.rs pin manifest entries (4 empty), dispositions (3 Independent + 1 StateProgressing), sections registration (case counts and section names), per-case verdicts, sentinel write/non-write contracts, probe-Err diagnostic format ('precondition' + 'remediation:'). Tests look up cases via rbtdrc_sections_for_fixture rather than calling fns directly — exercising the same dispatch path the engine uses, so a registration-without-implementation regression is caught. RCG-compliant: no pace/coronet references in comments, pub(crate) for cross-test consts (RBTDRL_SENTINEL_FILE, RBTDRL_OUTPUT_FILE) matching rbtdrk_canonical precedent, pub static for sections, internal case fns kept private. Locked decisions held: output.txt vs engine trace.txt distinction, no per-fixture tabtargets, no RBTDRM_SUITE_CALIBRANT const (suite-level fail-fast comes from existing rbte_engine.sh set -euo pipefail), hardcoded probe-Err deterministic. Build clean; 86/86 unit tests pass (69 prior + 17 new); fast TestSuite 72 fast-fixture passes + baseline rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels carry-forward (same as BBAAe). End-to-end binary dispatch verified: calibrant-verdicts fail-fasts after first fail, calibrant-sentinel writes both ran.sentinel and engine-auto trace.txt, calibrant-progressing exercises both probe paths.

0d4a6285  ₢BBAAc    Fix CRM v3 project displayName HTTP 400 surfaced by canonical-establish run from marshal-zero. RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON' → 'RBGC-DEPOT': underscores violate CRM v3 displayName allowed-character set (letters/digits/hyphen/space/single-quote/double-quote/exclamation per Google docs), and the resulting 'RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON canest2100000' was 31 chars exceeding the 30-char displayName ceiling. Either alone produces 'Request contains an invalid argument'. Single-constant fix flows to all five usage sites (depot project create, Mason SA create, Governor SA create, search anchor query, anchor-strip in moniker derivation) — load-bearing constant pattern earned. Mason/Governor SA displayNames go through IAM API (100-char limit, underscores allowed) so they wouldn't have failed; only the CRM v3 depot-project-create path surfaces the constraint. Updated the inline comment in zrbgp_depot_state_emit to reflect the new search query string. Specs reference the constant by name (RBSDE/RBSGM/RBSDL all use «RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX» placeholder) — no spec updates required. Verification: bash -n clean on both files; tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean; tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh 86/86 pass. Surprise captured per pristine-walkthrough wrap criterion: silent first-build assumption (BBAAm threaded the constant without exercising the actual CRM v3 create path), inline-fixed because single-constant scope, no follow-up pace required.

Surfaces DELETE_REQUESTED projects (previously invisible because billing is unlinked at unmake before soft-delete), eliminating the ₢BBAAc collision class where canonical-establish auto-pick chose monikers reserved by ghost soft-deleted projectIds. Adds RBGC_DEPOT_DISPLAY_PREFIX='RBGC_DEPOT_COMMON' as the load-bearing constant in rbgc_Constants.sh; threads it through three displayName creation sites in rbgp_Payor.sh (depot project, Mason SA, Governor SA — Governor's displayName previously had no moniker, now includes it for parity). Replaces zrbgp_depot_state_emit's billing-list + per-project SA-scan with a single CRM v3 projects:search call paginated by nextPageToken. State comes back in the search response (no separate CRM v1 lookup); moniker is derived by stripping the anchor + space prefix from displayName, regex-validated against ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$. RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID requirement removed from depot_list (still required at depot_levy for billing link). RBTDRK_FAMILY_STEM bumped 'canest' → 'canest2' to side-step pending-delete projectId reservations from burn-the-bridges teardown. content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — mimics operator onboarding journey under theurge control)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: crucible suite)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
75	
76	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
77	
78	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
79	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
80	
81	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
82	
83	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
84	
85	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
86	
87	## Suite-level fail-fast
88	
89	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
90	
91	## No-teardown decision
92	
93	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
94	
95	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
96	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
97	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
98	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
99	
100	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
101	
102	## Operator scope
103	
104	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
105	
106	## Coupling to ₣A_
107	
108	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
109	
110	## Locked design decisions — pointers
111	
112	- **Depot identity collapse** (BBAAM, landed): RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
113	- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
114	
115	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
116	
117	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
118	
119	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
120	
121	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
122	
123	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
124	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
125	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
126	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
127	
128	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
129	
130	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
131	
132	## References
133

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — mimics operator onboarding journey under theurge control)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: crucible suite)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions — pointers

- **Depot identity collapse** (BBAAM, landed): RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming** (BBAAN/BBAAQ/BBAAR, landed): SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAn

## Character

Surface eliminator: extract shared helper, add probe-mint behavior, validate. Mechanical bash refactor with one new behavior bolted in. Helper signature and module placement are mount-time calls.

## Wrap criterion

After the new helper returns, an immediate JWT-bearer mint with the freshly-written RBRA succeeds without consumer-side retry. Validated by `rbw-aM` re-mantle against an existing depot (fast iteration), then by `canonical-establish` from fresh marshal-zero — which then unblocks BBAAc retry.

## Locked decisions

- Single pace, full extraction. No refactor-then-probe split.
- Shared helper covers Governor (Payor mantle), Director (Governor invest), Retriever (Governor invest) — all three key-creation paths consolidated.
- Total probe budget: **90 seconds**. Poll cadence and backoff schedule are mount-time decisions.
- Produce-side guard only. RBRA on disk means usable for JWT-bearer mint. No consume-side fallback at the OAuth token-fetch site.
- Timeout = hard fail (`buc_die`). Pristine-tier contract; no partial-write tolerance, no degraded-RBRA shipping.
- Existing 404-retry-on-create logic preserved (orthogonal SA-write-path concern).

## Cross-pace dependencies

Blocks BBAAc pristine-walkthrough-manual: every retry from marshal-zero hits the same JWT-bearer race the moment Governor RBRA is consumed by the next case. Surface raised after BBAAm displayName fix landed (commit `0d4a6285`).

## Out of scope

- Empirical lag distribution measurement (operator chose to skip).
- Consume-side fallback at OAuth token-fetch.
- Divest paths (no key creation, no race).

content.text:
## Heat ₣BB — rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (23/30 racing)

**Paddock context.** Building `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third release tier whose entry contract refuses to run unless marshal-zero was just committed, so silent-first-build bugs get caught by construction. Composes a "gauntlet" TestSuite walking the state ladder from marshal-zero through canonical credentials to crucible verification.

**Recent work.** BBAAh calibrant fixture foundation, BBAAe gauntlet canonical-establish, BBAAm depot-list via displayName search (raised the 30-char ceiling spook), BBAAc's first round closed with the CRM v3 displayName fix (commit `0d4a6285`).

## Next pace — ₢BBAAn sa-key-propagation-probe [rough]

**The surface.** Freshly-written RBRA on disk isn't immediately usable for JWT-bearer mint — there's a propagation race after SA key creation. Every canonical-establish retry from marshal-zero hits this when the next case consumes the just-created Governor key.

**Goal.** Single shared helper covering all three key-creation paths (Governor via Payor mantle, Director invest, Retriever invest). After the helper returns, the RBRA is *guaranteed* mintable. 90s probe budget; timeout = `buc_die` (pristine contract — no degraded-RBRA shipping).

**Silks fit.** "sa-key-propagation-probe" describes the work cleanly. No rename.

**Approach (proposal):**
1. Locate the three SA-key-creation sites — Governor in `rbgp_Payor.sh` (zrbgp_governor_mantle path), Director and Retriever in `rbgg_Governor.sh` (invest functions). Existing 404-retry-on-create stays put.
2. Survey `rbgv_AccessProbe.sh` for the JWT-bearer mint primitive — that's the probe mechanism we drive in a loop.
3. Pick helper home + signature. Candidates: new function in `rbgi_IAM.sh` (key-creation lives there), or in `rbgg_Governor.sh` (Governor is the orchestrator). Mount-time call; will recommend after reading the modules.
4. Wire the three call sites to the shared helper, run `bash -n` + `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` + `tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh` (86 cases).
5. Validate: `tt/rbw-aM.PayorMantlesGovernor.sh` against existing depot (fast iteration), then `canonical-establish` fixture from fresh marshal-zero.

**Execution strategy.** Sequential, sonnet-tier — bash refactor across 2–3 modules with one coherent new behavior. No parallelization (file edits interleave; helper signature drives all three call sites). Inline-arm sonnet rather than escalating to opus.

Ready to proceed with module inspection, or do you want to redirect on helper placement first?

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Governor - Project Orchestration
20	
21	
22	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
23	# Operational Invariants (RBGG is single writer; 409 is fatal)
24	#
25	# - Single admin actor: All RBGG operations are executed by a single admin
26	#   identity. There are no concurrent writers in the same project.
27	# - Pristine-state expectation: RBGG init/creation flows assume the project
28	#   is pristine for the resources they manage. If a resource "already exists"
29	#   (HTTP 409), that's treated as state drift or prior manual activity.
30	# - Policy: All HTTP 409 Conflict responses are fatal (buc_die). We do not
31	#   treat 409 as idempotent success anywhere in RBGG.
32	#   If you see a 409, resolve state drift first (destroy/reset), then rerun.
33	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	set -euo pipefail
36	
37	# Multiple inclusion detection
38	test -z "${ZRBGG_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
39	ZRBGG_SOURCED=1
40	
41	######################################################################
42	# Internal Functions (zrbgg_*)
43	
44	zrbgg_kindle() {
45	  test -z "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg already kindled"
46	
47	  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}"     || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
48	  test   "${#RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" -gt 0 || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is empty"
49	
50	  buc_log_args 'Ensure dependencies are kindled first'
51	  zrbgc_sentinel
52	  zrbgo_sentinel
53	  zrbgu_sentinel
54	  zrbgi_sentinel
55	
56	  readonly ZRBGG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_"
57	  readonly ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGG_PREFIX}empty.json"
58	  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON}"
59	
60	  # Infix values for HTTP operations
61	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE="create"
62	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY="verify"
63	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY="key"
64	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_IAM_ENABLE="api_iam_enable"
65	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CRM_ENABLE="api_crm_enable"
66	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_ART_ENABLE="api_art_enable"
67	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_BUILD_ENABLE="api_build_enable"
68	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_SA_ACCOUNT_GEN="cb_account_gen"
69	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_PRIME="cb_prime"
70	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CONTAINERANALYSIS_ENABLE="api_containeranalysis_enable"
71	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_STORAGE_ENABLE="api_storage_enable"
72	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
73	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
74	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
75	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_REPO="delete_repo"
76	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_REPO_POLICY="repo_policy"
77	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_RPOLICY_SET="repo_policy_set"
78	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER="roster"
79	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
80	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE="delete"
81	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS="list_keys"
82	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
83	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE="bucket_delete"
84	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST="bucket_list"
85	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE="object_delete"
86	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
87	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
88	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
89	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
90	
91	  readonly ZRBGG_KINDLED=1
92	}
93	
94	zrbgg_sentinel() {
95	  test "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgg not kindled - call zrbgg_kindle first"
96	}
97	
98	# Path-driven sibling of rbgu_json_field_capture for files that live outside
99	# the rbgu cache (under RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/* for credential bytes that
100	# must not leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR).
101	zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture() {
102	  zrbgg_sentinel
103	  local -r z_path="${1}"
104	  local -r z_jq="${2}"
105	  local z_result
106	  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_path}")  || return 1
107	  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
108	  echo "${z_result}"
109	}
110	
111	######################################################################
112	######################################################################
113	# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
114	zrbgg_required_apis_missing_capture() {
115	  zrbgg_sentinel
116	
117	  local z_token="${1:-}"
118	  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }
119	
120	  local z_missing=""
121	  local z_api=""
122	  local z_service=""
123	  local z_infix=""
124	  local z_state=""
125	  local z_code=""
126	
127	  for z_api in                       \
128	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
129	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
130	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
131	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
132	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
133	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
134	  do
135	    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
136	    z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"
137	
138	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true
139	
140	    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
141	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
142	    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1
143	
144	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
145	      z_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
146	      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
147	    else
148	      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'
149	      z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
150	    fi
151	  done
152	
153	  printf '%s' "${z_missing# }"
154	}
155	
156	zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
157	  zrbgg_sentinel
158	
159	  local z_account_name="$1"
160	  local z_display_name="$2"
161	  local z_description="$3"
162	  local z_role="$4"
163	
164	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
165	
166	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
167	  local z_token
168	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
169	
170	  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
171	  jq -n                                      \
172	    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
173	    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
174	    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
175	    '{
176	      accountId: $account_id,
177	      serviceAccount: {
178	        displayName: $display_name,
179	        description: $description
180	      }
181	    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"
182	
183	  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
184	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
185	    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
186	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"
187	
188	  local z_sa_uid
189	  z_sa_uid=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
190	    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
191	  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"
192	
193	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
194	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"
195	
196	  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'
197	
198	  buc_log_args 'List keys'
199	  rbgu_http_json "GET"                                                        \
200	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}"        \
201	                                      "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"
202	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service account keys" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"
203	
204	  buc_log_args 'Count existing user-managed keys'
205	  local z_user_keys
206	  z_user_keys=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}" \
207	                 '[.keys[]? | select(.keyType=="USER_MANAGED")] | length') \
208	    || buc_die "Failed to parse service account keys"
209	
210	  if test "${z_user_keys}" -gt 0; then
211	    buc_log_args 'Provide a console URL to delete keys manually, then rerun this command'
212	    local z_sa_email_enc="${z_account_email//@/%40}"
213	    local z_keys_url="${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}iam-admin/serviceaccounts/details/${z_sa_email_enc}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
214	
215	    buc_warn "Found ${z_user_keys} existing USER_MANAGED key(s) on ${z_account_email}."
216	    buc_info "Open Console, select the **Keys** tab, delete old keys, then rerun:"
217	    buc_info "  ${z_keys_url}"
218	    buc_die  "Aborting to avoid minting additional keys."
219	  fi
220	
221	  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
222	  local z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_request.json"
223	  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"
224	
225	  local z_key_attempt=0
226	  local z_key_infix=""
227	  local z_key_code=""
228	  while :; do
229	    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))
230	    z_key_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY}-attempt${z_key_attempt}"
231	    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}" \
232	                                           "${z_token}" "${z_key_infix}" "${z_key_req}"
233	
234	    z_key_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_key_infix}") || z_key_code=""
235	
236	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "200"; then
237	      break
238	    fi
239	
240	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "404" && test "${z_key_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
241	      buc_warn "keys.create returned 404 (SA write-path propagation delay), retry ${z_key_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
242	      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
243	      continue
244	    fi
245	
246	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Generate service account key" "${z_key_infix}"
247	  done
248	
249	  buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
250	  local z_key_b64
251	  z_key_b64=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
252	    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
253	  # Decode into the assay subdirectory (RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/) so the
254	  # only readable form of the private key shares lifecycle and location
255	  # with the final RBRA file — credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR.
256	  local -r z_assay_dir="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE%/*}"
257	  local -r z_key_json="${z_assay_dir}/_decoded_key_${z_account_name}.json"
258	  printf '%s' "${z_key_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_key_json}" \
259	    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"
260	
261	  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
262	  local z_rbra_file="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
263	
264	  local z_client_email
265	  z_client_email=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.client_email') \
266	    || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email from key JSON"
267	
268	  local z_private_key
269	  z_private_key=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.private_key') \
270	    || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key from key JSON"
271	
272	  local z_project_id
273	  z_project_id=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.project_id') \
274	    || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id from key JSON"
275	
276	  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
277	  {
278	    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n'                  "${z_role}"
279	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "$z_client_email"
280	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "$z_private_key"; printf '"\n'
281	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "$z_project_id"
282	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
283	  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
284	
285	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
286	
287	  # Decoded JSON lives in RBRR_SECRETS_DIR (not BURD_TEMP_DIR), so
288	  # BCG:518's no-module-temp-deletion rule does not bind. Remove now
289	  # that the bytes are persisted in RBRA form.
290	  rm -f "${z_key_json}"
291	
292	  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"
293	
294	  # Echo uniqueId to stdout for callers (all logging goes to stderr per BCG)
295	  printf '%s' "${z_sa_uid}"
296	}

298	zrbgg_create_gcs_bucket() {
299	  zrbgg_sentinel
300	
301	  local z_token="${1}"
302	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
303	
304	  buc_log_args 'Create bucket request JSON for '"${z_bucket_name}"
305	  local z_bucket_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_bucket_create_req.json"
306	  jq -n --arg name "${z_bucket_name}" --arg location "${RBGC_GAR_LOCATION}" '
307	{
308	  name: $name,
309	  location: $location,
310	  storageClass: "STANDARD",
311	  lifecycle: { rule: [ { action: { type: "Delete" }, condition: { age: 1 } } ] }
312	}' > "${z_bucket_req}" || buc_die "Failed to create bucket request JSON"
313	
314	  buc_log_args 'Send bucket creation request'
315	  local z_code
316	  local z_err
317	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_token}" \
318	                                  "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_bucket_req}"
319	  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
320	  z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"
321	
322	  case "${z_code}" in
323	    200|201) buc_info "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} created";                    return 0 ;;
324	    409)     buc_die  "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} already exists (pristine-state violation)" ;;
325	    *)       buc_die  "Failed to create bucket: ${z_err}"                             ;;
326	  esac
327	}

329	zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture() {
330	  zrbgg_sentinel
331	
332	  local z_token="${1}"
333	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
334	
335	  local z_list_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o"
336	  local z_page_token=""
337	  local z_first=1
338	
339	  while :; do
340	    buc_log_args "Build URL with optional pageToken -> ${z_first}"
341	    local z_url="${z_list_url_base}"
342	    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
343	      buc_log_args 'pageToken must be URL-encoded'
344	      local z_tok_enc
345	      z_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") || return 1
346	      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
347	    fi
348	
349	    buc_log_args 'Use a unique infix per page to avoid clobbering files'
350	    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST}${z_first}"
351	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
352	
353	    local z_code
354	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
355	    test "${z_code}" = "200" || return 1
356	
357	    buc_log_args 'Print names from this page (if any)'
358	    buc_log_args 'Next page?'
359	    jq -r                '.items[]?.name // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"  || return 1
360	    z_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.nextPageToken') || z_page_token=""
361	
362	    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
363	    z_first=$((z_first + 1))
364	  done
365	}

383	zrbgg_empty_gcs_bucket() {
384	  zrbgg_sentinel
385	
386	  local z_token="${1}"
387	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
388	
389	  buc_log_args 'Get list of objects to delete'
390	  local z_objects
391	  z_objects=$(zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture "${z_token}" "${z_bucket_name}") || {
392	    buc_log_args 'No objects found or bucket not accessible'
393	    return 0
394	  }
395	
396	  test -n "${z_objects}" || { buc_log_args 'Bucket is empty'; return 0; }
397	
398	  buc_log_args 'Delete each object'
399	  local z_object=""
400	  local z_delete_url=""
401	  local z_delete_code=""
402	  while IFS= read -r z_object; do
403	    test -n "${z_object}" || continue
404	    buc_log_args "Deleting object: ${z_object}"
405	
406	    local z_object_enc
407	    z_object_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_object}") || z_object_enc=""
408	    test -n "${z_object_enc}" || { buc_warn "Failed to encode object name: ${z_object}"; continue; }
409	    z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o/${z_object_enc}"
410	
411	    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" \
412	                              "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}"
413	    z_delete_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}") || z_delete_code=""
414	    case "${z_delete_code}" in
415	      204|404) buc_log_args "Object ${z_object}: deleted or not found"                     ;;
416	      *)       buc_warn     "Object ${z_object}: Failed to delete (HTTP ${z_delete_code})" ;;
417	    esac
418	  done <<< "${z_objects}"
419	}

443	  esac

446	######################################################################
447	# External Functions (rbgg_*)
448	
449	# Roster a single role: paginate the SA list, filter by role-prefix,
450	# emit per-identity fact files (basename = identity, content = full email)
451	# and a human-readable line per match. Used by rbgg_roster_retrievers /
452	# rbgg_roster_directors via $1 = role tinder, $2 = fact-extension tinder.
453	zrbgg_roster_role() {
454	  zrbgg_sentinel
455	
456	  local -r z_role="$1"
457	  local -r z_fact_ext="$2"
458	
459	  buc_step "Rostering ${z_role} service accounts in project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
460	
461	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
462	  local z_token
463	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"
464	
465	  local z_url_base="${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}"
466	  local z_page_token=""
467	  local z_page=1
468	  local z_count=0
469	
470	  while :; do
471	    local z_url="${z_url_base}"
472	    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
473	      local z_tok_enc
474	      z_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") \
475	        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode pageToken"
476	      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
477	    fi
478	
479	    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER}_${z_role}_${z_page}"
480	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
481	    rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts (page ${z_page})" "${z_infix}"
482	
483	    local z_sa_count
484	    z_sa_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
485	      || buc_die "Failed to parse service accounts page"
486	
487	    local z_index=0
488	    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
489	      local z_sa_email
490	      z_sa_email=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".accounts[${z_index}].email") \
491	        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
492	      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${z_role}-(.+)@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}$ ]]; then
493	        local z_identity="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
494	        buf_write_fact_multi "${z_identity}" "${z_fact_ext}" "${z_sa_email}"
495	        buc_bare "  ${z_identity}  ${z_sa_email}"
496	        z_count=$((z_count + 1))
497	      fi
498	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
499	    done
500	
501	    z_page_token=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.nextPageToken') || z_page_token=""
502	    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
503	    z_page=$((z_page + 1))
504	  done
505	
506	  buc_info "Found ${z_count} ${z_role} service account(s)"
507	  buc_success "Roster operation completed"
508	}

577	rbgg_invest_director() {
578	  zrbgg_sentinel
579	
580	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
581	
582	  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Director service account for an identity"
583	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_role_director}-<identity>"
584	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
585	
586	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
587	
588	  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_director}-${z_identity}"
589	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
590	
591	  buc_step "Investing Director service account: ${z_account_name}"
592	
593	  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                      \
594	    "${z_account_name}"                                    \
595	    "Recipe Bottle Director (${z_identity})"               \
596	    "Create/destroy container images for ${z_identity}"    \
597	    "${RBCC_role_director}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Director SA"
598	
599	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
600	  local z_token
601	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
602	
603	  buc_step 'Adding Cloud Build Editor role (project scope)'
604	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
605	    "${z_token}"                            \
606	    "Grant Cloud Build Editor"              \
607	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
608	    "${RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR}" \
609	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
610	    "director-cb"
611	
612	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
613	    "${z_token}"                            \
614	    "Grant Project Viewer"                  \
615	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
616	    "roles/viewer"                          \
617	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
618	    "director-viewer"
619	
620	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
621	    "${z_token}"                            \
622	    "Grant Worker Pool User"               \
623	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
624	    "roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser"       \
625	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
626	    "director-pool"
627	
628	  buc_step 'Grant serviceAccountUser on Mason'
629	  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "${z_account_email}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser"
630	
631	  buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
632	  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
633	  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
634	  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
635	  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
636	  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"
637	
638	  local z_gar_prop_delay=3
639	  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
640	  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=420
641	  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
642	  local z_gar_get_infix=""
643	  local z_gar_set_infix=""
644	  local z_gar_get_code=""
645	  local z_gar_get_err=""
646	
647	  while :; do
648	    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
649	    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
650	    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
651	
652	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
653	    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""
654	
655	    # Propagation retry: newly-created Director SA may not be visible yet
656	    if test "${z_gar_get_code}" = "400"; then
657	      z_gar_get_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_err=""
658	      case "${z_gar_get_err}" in
659	        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
660	          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
661	            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
662	          buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
663	          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
664	          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
665	          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
666	          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
667	          continue
668	          ;;
669	      esac
670	    fi
671	
672	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
673	
674	    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
675	    local z_gar_partial
676	    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
677	      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
678	      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"
679	
680	    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
681	    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
682	      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"
683	
684	    local z_gar_complete
685	    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
686	      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
687	      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"
688	
689	    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
690	    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
691	      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
692	    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"
693	
694	    local z_gar_set_code
695	    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"
696	
697	    # Propagation retry on SET
698	    if test "${z_gar_set_code}" = "400"; then
699	      local z_gar_set_err=""
700	      z_gar_set_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || z_gar_set_err=""
701	      case "${z_gar_set_err}" in
702	        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
703	          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
704	            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
705	          buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
706	          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
707	          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
708	          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
709	          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
710	          continue
711	          ;;
712	      esac
713	    fi
714	
715	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
716	    break
717	  done
718	
719	  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
720	  buc_info ""
721	  buc_info "Install:"
722	  buc_bare "        cp ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
723	}
724	
725	# Divest a single role+identity: synthesize email, DELETE (404-tolerant),
726	# opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup only on email match.
727	zrbgg_divest_role() {
728	  zrbgg_sentinel
729	
730	  local -r z_role="$1"
731	  local -r z_identity="$2"
732	  local -r z_role_rbra_file="$3"
733	
734	  local z_account_email="${z_role}-${z_identity}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
735	
736	  buc_step "Divesting service account: ${z_account_email}"
737	
738	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
739	  local z_token
740	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
741	
742	  buc_log_args 'Delete via REST API (404-tolerant)'
743	  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
744	                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}"
745	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}" \
746	    404 "not found (already deleted)"
747	
748	  buc_log_args 'Opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup if installed credential matches'
749	  if test -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"; then
750	    local z_installed_email
751	    z_installed_email=$(. "${z_role_rbra_file}" 2>/dev/null && printf '%s' "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}") \
752	      || z_installed_email=""
753	    if test "${z_installed_email}" = "${z_account_email}"; then
754	      rm -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"
755	      buc_info "Removed installed RBRA at ${z_role_rbra_file} (matched divested email)"
756	    fi
757	  fi
758	
759	  buc_success "Divest operation completed"
760	}
761	
762	rbgg_divest_retriever() {
763	  zrbgg_sentinel
764	
765	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
766	
767	  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Retriever service account by identity"
768	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_role_retriever}-<identity>"
769	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
770	
771	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
772	
773	  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_role_retriever}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
774	}
775	
776	rbgg_divest_director() {
777	  zrbgg_sentinel
778	
779	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
780	
781	  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Director service account by identity"
782	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_role_director}-<identity>"
783	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
784	
785	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
786	
787	  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_role_director}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
788	}
789	
790	rbgg_destroy_project() {
791	  zrbgg_sentinel
792	  buc_doc_brief "DEPRECATED: Use rbgp_project_delete instead - moved to Payor module for billing/destructive ops"
793	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
794	
795	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
796	  buc_warn "DEPRECATION NOTICE: rbgg_destroy_project is deprecated"
797	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
798	  buc_warn ""
799	  buc_warn "Project deletion has been moved to the Payor module which handles"
800	  buc_warn "all billing and destructive lifecycle operations."
801	  buc_warn ""
802	  buc_warn "Use instead:"
803	  buc_warn "  rbgp_project_delete - Full project deletion with proper safeguards"
804	  buc_warn ""
805	  buc_warn "The Payor module provides additional features like lien management,"
806	  buc_warn "billing detachment, and project restoration capabilities."
807	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
808	
809	  buc_die "Function moved to Payor module - use rbgp_project_delete"
810	
811	  if [[ "${DEBUG_ONLY:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
812	    buc_die "This dangerous operation requires DEBUG_ONLY=1 environment variable"
813	  fi
814	
815	  buc_step 'Mint admin OAuth token'
816	  local z_token
817	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
818	
819	  buc_step 'Triple confirmation required'
820	  buc_warn ""
821	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
822	  buc_warn "CRITICAL WARNING: You are about to PERMANENTLY DELETE the entire project:"
823	  buc_warn "  Project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
824	  buc_warn "This will:"
825	  buc_warn "  - Delete ALL resources in the project"
826	  buc_warn "  - Delete ALL data permanently"
827	  buc_warn "  - Break billing associations"
828	  buc_warn "  - Make the project unusable immediately"
829	  buc_warn "  - Cannot be undone after 30-day grace period"
830	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
831	  buc_warn ""
832	
833	  buc_require "Type the exact project ID to confirm deletion" "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
834	  buc_require "Confirm you understand this DELETES EVERYTHING in the project" "DELETE-EVERYTHING"
835	  buc_require "Final confirmation - type OBLITERATE to proceed" "OBLITERATE"
836	
837	  buc_step 'Check for liens (will block deletion)'
838	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
839	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List liens" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
840	
841	  local z_lien_count
842	  z_lien_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
843	
844	  if [[ "${z_lien_count}" -gt 0 ]]; then
845	    buc_step 'BLOCKED: Liens exist on project'
846	    buc_warn "Project has ${z_lien_count} lien(s) that prevent deletion"
847	    buc_warn "You must remove all liens first:"
848	    buc_code "  gcloud resource-manager liens list --project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
849	    buc_code "  gcloud resource-manager liens delete LIEN_NAME --project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
850	    buc_warn "Then re-run this command."
851	    buc_die "Cannot proceed with active liens"
852	  fi
853	
854	  buc_step 'Delete project (immediate lifecycle change to DELETE_REQUESTED)'
855	  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${RBGD_API_CRM_DELETE_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE}"
856	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Delete project" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE}"
857	
858	  buc_step 'Verify deletion state'
859	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
860	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Get project state" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
861	
862	  local z_lifecycle_state
863	  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
864	
865	  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
866	    buc_success "Project successfully marked for deletion"
867	    buc_step "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
868	    buc_step "Grace period: Up to 30 days"
869	    buc_code "To restore (if still possible): rbgg_restore_project"
870	    buc_step "WARNING: Project is now unusable but may remain visible in listings"
871	  else
872	    buc_die "Unexpected project state after deletion: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
873	  fi
874	}
875	
876	rbgg_restore_project() {
877	  zrbgg_sentinel
878	  buc_doc_brief "Attempt to restore a deleted project within the 30-day grace period"
879	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
880	
881	  buc_step 'Mint admin OAuth token'
882	  local z_token
883	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
884	
885	  buc_step 'Check current project state'
886	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
887	
888	  if ! rbgu_http_is_ok "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"; then
889	    buc_die "Cannot access project - it may have been permanently deleted or never existed"
890	  fi
891	
892	  local z_lifecycle_state
893	  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
894	
895	  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
896	    buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only restore projects in DELETE_REQUESTED state"
897	  fi
898	
899	  buc_step 'Confirm restoration'
900	  buc_log_args "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
901	  buc_log_args "Attempting to restore project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
902	  buc_log_args "WARNING: Restore may fail if deletion process has already started"
903	  buc_require "Confirm restoration of project" "RESTORE"
904	
905	  buc_step 'Attempt project restoration'
906	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_CRM_UNDELETE_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}"
907	  if rbgu_http_is_ok                                                    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}"; then
908	    buc_step 'Verify restoration'
909	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
910	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get restored project state"               "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
911	
912	    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse restored project state"
913	
914	    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
915	      buc_success "Project successfully restored to ACTIVE state"
916	      buc_log_args "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
917	      buc_log_args "Project is now usable again"
918	    else
919	      buc_die "Restoration completed but project state is unexpected: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
920	    fi
921	  else
922	    local z_error_msg
923	    z_error_msg=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}" '.error.message // "Unknown error"') || z_error_msg="Failed to parse error"
924	    buc_die "Project restoration failed: ${z_error_msg}"
925	  fi
926	}
927	
928	# eof
929	
930
